notes: Tests whether the skill produces scroll-stopping hooks (not generic AI-ad copy). Grades each hook individually on length, specificity, absence of AI-slop patterns, and presence of psychological triggers.

universal_assertions.text: Output contains at least 10 distinct hooks
No hooks start with 'Are you...' (tired generic AI ad opener)
Hooks include concrete specifics (numbers, names, objects, situations) not vague claims
Output uses VARIETY of psychological trigger types (not all the same category)
Hooks feel native to the given persona's voice/context (not generic ad copy)

evals.name: problem-aware-sleep-supplement
evals.prompt: Write 10 video hooks for a sleep supplement brand. Product: a magnesium + l-theanine drink mix you add to water 30 min before bed. Pain: you wake up at 3am and can't get back to sleep. Persona: burned-out knowledge workers, 35-50, high-cortisol, working parents. Awareness stage: Problem-Aware (knows they sleep poorly, tried melatonin, nothing sticks). Format: 3-second spoken video hooks. I want hooks that agitate the pain HARD and make them feel deeply understood — not generic 'are you tired of bad sleep?' nonsense.
evals.context: Tests problem-awareness stage (agitation), specificity to 3am-waking scenario, absence of generic 'are you tired' openers.

fixture_assertions.text: Multiple hooks specifically reference the 3am-waking pattern (not generic 'bad sleep')
Hooks SHOW the pain (scene, moment, sensation) rather than TELLING it ('you're tired')

evals.name: solution-aware-project-mgmt-saas
evals.prompt: Write 10 static text hooks for a project management SaaS. Product: lightweight PM tool for creative agencies, one-pane view, no 47-layer permissions. Pain: creatives hate Asana/Monday — too much clicking, too bureaucratic. Persona: creative directors and founders at boutique design/brand agencies (5-15 people). Awareness stage: Solution-Aware (already using one of the big tools, frustrated). Format: text hooks for Instagram carousel first-slide. I want hooks that call out what Asana/Monday got wrong — differentiation, not just claims of being better.
evals.context: Tests solution-awareness (differentiation), naming competitors, specific UX gripes (clicking, permissions), absence of generic 'better software' claims.

fixture_assertions.text: Hooks specifically reference Asana/Monday by name OR concrete UX gripes (clicking, permissions, 47 layers)
Hooks differentiate (show what competitors got wrong), not just claim to be better

evals.name: unaware-therapy-for-founders
evals.prompt: Write 10 video hooks for a therapy service that specializes in founders and entrepreneurs. Product: 1:1 therapy, weekly 50-min sessions, therapists who've worked with VC-backed founders. Pain: founder loneliness, decision fatigue, identity fusion with the company. Persona: early-stage founders, 28-40, Series A or bootstrapped, working 60+ hr weeks. Awareness stage: UNAWARE — they don't think they have a mental health problem, they think they're just 'stressed.' Format: first-person spoken video hooks. I want hooks that introduce the pain through a relatable situation — no product mention, no therapy mention, no 'do you feel' openers.
evals.context: Tests unaware-stage strategy (situation-led, no product mention), specific founder moments (decision fatigue, identity fusion), absence of 'do you feel' template.

fixture_assertions.text: Hooks do NOT mention therapy, mental health, or the product directly (unaware stage)
Hooks name specific founder situations (Slack at midnight, decision fatigue, board calls, cap tables, etc.)
No hooks use 'Do you feel...' template opener
